senses.definition: to mature mentally and not do certain behaviors

examples.x: As he aged out, he developed a better understanding of the complexities of life.
With experience and patience, one's perspective on certain matters can age out and become more insightful.

senses.definition: to bring to equality by adjusting different elements

examples.x: We aim to balance out opportunities for everyone in the community.
Let's balance out the distribution of snacks for the meeting.
The government is working to balance out educational resources across regions.
The organization is determined to balance out access to healthcare services.
He balanced out the playing time among the team members.

senses.definition: to sing loudly and boldly, expressing strong musical energy

examples.x: He surprised everyone when he started to belt out a rock ballad.
The vocal coach taught her how to belt out high notes effortlessly.
The street performer could be heard belting out tunes from a distance.

senses.definition: to become dry or drier after the removal of moisture

examples.x: The soil will eventually dry out after a few days of no rainfall.
Hang the wet towels outside, and they will gradually dry out in the breeze.
After the rain, the flooded streets started to dry out under the warmth of the sun.
Leave the freshly painted artwork to dry out completely before framing it.
Wet paint on the walls will slowly dry out, revealing the true color.

senses.definition: to reach a point where growth or upward movement ceases, resulting in a stable or consistent level

examples.x: After several years of rapid expansion, the company's profits began to flatten out.
The stock market's upward trend finally flattened out, leading to stability in investment values.
Her weight loss progress flattened out, and she maintained a steady, healthy weight.
In recent years, the demand for the product has flattened out, resulting in stable sales figures.
The housing market prices have flattened out, making it a more affordable time to buy a home.

senses.definition: to turn from a liquid state into a solid state as a result of exposure to low temperatures

examples.x: I didn't expect the material to freeze out so quickly in these frigid conditions.
The freezing point of this chemical compound is so low that it doesn't freeze out in most climates.
If you leave the soda in the freezer too long, it might freeze out and burst the container.
In sub-zero temperatures, engine oil can start to freeze out, leading to engine problems.
Water freezes out when the temperature drops below freezing.

senses.definition: to live in a location separate from one's primary place of activity

examples.x: The student lived out in a nearby apartment complex rather than staying in the university dorms.
The young professional lived out in a suburban neighborhood, commuting to their downtown office.
The factory workers lived out in the surrounding towns, traveling to the plant each day.
The students lived out in a homestay arrangement, immersing themselves in the local culture.
The doctor lived out in the countryside, providing medical care to rural communities.

senses.definition: to produce a paper copy of a document from a printer

examples.x: I need to print out these important documents for the conference.
The software allows you to easily print out photos.
Before the workshop, make sure to print out the handouts.

senses.definition: to exit a computer account such as email, Instagram, etc. in a way that you have to re-enter your username and password in order to use it again

examples.x: Don't forget to sign out when you're done using the computer.
He accidentally left the account signed in and had to sign out remotely.
The security policy requires employees to sign out before leaving the office.

senses.definition: to gradually become unfrozen after being taken out of the freezer

examples.x: I need to thaw out the frozen vegetables for tonight's meal.
Don't forget to thaw out the frozen berries for breakfast.

senses.definition: to exercise in order to get healthier or stronger

examples.x: I work out with a friend to make the exercise routine more enjoyable.
We need to find time to work out together and motivate each other.
Despite a busy schedule, he manages to work out regularly to maintain his fitness levels.
I try to work out at least three times a week for better cardiovascular health.
She worked out for an hour yesterday after work.

senses.definition: to adjust the lens of a camera in a way that makes the person or thing being filmed or photographed appear further away or smaller

examples.x: The photographer skillfully zoomed out, revealing the breathtaking expanse of the sunset.

senses.definition: to complete or conduct a task, job, etc.

examples.x: The research team will carry out experiments to test the hypothesis and gather data.
It is essential to carry out a thorough analysis of market trends before launching a new product.
The detective was assigned to carry out the investigation into the mysterious disappearance.
The technician will carry out routine maintenance on the machinery to prevent any potential issues.
Before making a decision, it's crucial to carry out a cost-benefit analysis of the proposed changes.

senses.definition: to decorate or arrange something, typically a place, in a particular way

examples.x: They decided to do out the venue with colorful decorations for the festive celebration.
Before the event, the team worked to do out the conference room with banners and posters.
She wanted to do out her new apartment in a modern and stylish design.
The team was eager to do out the office space to create a welcoming and inspiring environment.

senses.definition: to resolve problems or disagreements through discussion or effort to reach a solution or agreement

examples.x: The team needed to have a meeting to iron out the details of the project.
The two countries have been trying to iron out their trade disputes for months.
Last year, they ironed out their differences and established a successful partnership.

senses.definition: to put or organize things in a tidy or systematic way

examples.x: After the move, she spent the weekend sorting out her belongings in the new apartment.
The librarian decided to sort out the books on the shelves to make it easier for patrons to find them.
He began to sort out the files on his computer to create a more efficient digital workspace.

senses.definition: to record the time of one's departure from work

examples.x: Every day, employees must remember to clock out before leaving the office to ensure accurate attendance records.
It's important to clock out on time to avoid any issues with payroll and to comply with company policies.
If you forget to clock out, it may affect your overtime calculations and cause confusion in the attendance records.
The electronic system makes it easy for employees to clock out with a simple swipe of their ID cards.
Due to the flexibility of the job, remote workers often appreciate the ability to clock out after completing their tasks rather than adhering to a strict schedule.

senses.definition: to complete an official form or document by writing information on it

examples.x: Please take a moment to fill out the application form for the job.
Students are required to fill out the registration form before the start of the semester.
Make sure to fill out all the sections of the medical history form at your appointment.
Before your visit, you'll need to fill out the consent form for the medical procedure.
Participants were asked to fill out a questionnaire to provide feedback on the training program.

senses.definition: to write something on paper, ensuring it is clear and includes all the necessary details

examples.x: Please write out your answers neatly on the exam sheet.
The professor wrote out the assignment details on the board.

senses.definition: (of an emotion or action) to be in control and very strong at the moment

examples.x: Love and understanding should always win out over hatred and resentment.
Rational thinking usually wins out when making important decisions.
Let's not let fear win out over our determination to succeed.
Sometimes, curiosity wins out over fear, leading to new discoveries.
Compassion often wins out, fostering better understanding among people.

senses.definition: to put out a flame, candle, etc. using the air in one's lungs
to suddenly and forcefully break and release what is inside

examples.x: The watermelon burst out when he dropped it on the floor.
Please burst out the bubbles from the bubble wrap before packing.
The pressure caused the soda can to burst out, spraying liquid everywhere.
The volcano erupted, causing lava to burst out from the crater.
The dam burst out, flooding the valley with water.

senses.definition: to make something stop burning or shining

examples.x: Please put out the campfire before leaving the site.
He forgot to put out his cigarette, causing a small burn on the carpet.
The wind put out the lanterns on the porch.

senses.definition: to take control of a company or business by purchasing all its shares

examples.x: The energy company has expressed interest in buying out the renewable energy startup.
The investment firm has a plan to buy out a majority stake in the telecommunications company.
The real estate developer aims to buy out the prime commercial property for a new project.
The tech company's aggressive strategy is to buy out innovative startups in the industry.

senses.definition: to get money in exchange for selling something valuable one owns

examples.x: I'm going to cash out these unused gift cards.
You can cash out your loyalty points for discounts.
I decided to cash out my old video games to get some extra money.
We can cash out these tokens for prizes.
She plans to cash out her stocks before the market takes a downturn.

senses.definition: to spend a lot of money on fancy or unnecessary things

examples.x: After completing a challenging project, the team decided to splash out on a spa day to relax and rejuvenate.
The family decided to splash out on a new car for a more comfortable and enjoyable travel experience.
To celebrate their anniversary, the couple splashed out on a gourmet dinner and a weekend getaway.
The entrepreneur splashed out on a lavish office space to create a motivating work environment.
To mark the end of exams, the students decided to splash out on a fancy dinner to celebrate their accomplishments.
